report_name: Deutsche Bank Confirms Vendor Breach After 'Unsafe' Ransomware Claim

threat_actors.name: Unsafe
threat_actors.description: A relatively new ransomware group that claimed a breach against Deutsche Bank by compromising a third-party marketing vendor in Germany and leaking stolen data on its dark web site.

attack_patterns.name: Supply Chain Compromise
attack_patterns.description: The Unsafe ransomware group compromised a third-party marketing vendor to indirectly access data belonging to Deutsche Bank, representing a classic supply chain attack.

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Relationship
attack_patterns.description: The attackers exploited the trusted relationship between Deutsche Bank and its external marketing vendor to gain access to sensitive employee data.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: The Unsafe group collected data related to Deutsche Bank from the compromised marketing and incentive platform, which acted as an information repository.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The attackers exfiltrated stolen data and posted it on their dark web leak site to publicize the breach and extort the victim.

malware.name: Unsafe Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware associated with the Unsafe group, used in an attack against a third-party vendor of Deutsche Bank. The group exfiltrated data and posted it to a leak site as part of their extortion tactics.

identities.name: Deutsche Bank
identities.description: A global financial services firm whose employee data was compromised via a breach at a third-party marketing vendor. The bank's internal systems were not directly breached.

identities.name: Unnamed German Marketing Vendor
identities.description: The third-party service provider for Deutsche Bank that was directly breached by the Unsafe ransomware group, leading to the exfiltration of Deutsche Bank employee data from a marketing and incentive platform.

infrastructure.name: Unsafe Ransomware Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the Unsafe ransomware group to publish data allegedly stolen from Deutsche Bank's vendor to extort the victim and gain notoriety.

indicators.name: Anomalous Third-Party Vendor Logon
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Third-Party Vendor Logon
id: 7b9c1d4e-3f2a-4b8e-9c1d-4e3f2a1b8e9c
status: experimental
description: 'Monitors for anomalous login patterns from third-party vendor accounts, such as logins from unusual geolocations or outside of business hours, which could indicate a supply chain compromise.'
references:
  - https://www.cyberdaily.au/security/13907-major-german-bank-says-a-third-party-may-have-been-hit-following-claims-of-ransomware-attack
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-16
tags:
  - attack.t1199
  - attack.initial_access
level: medium
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: signinlogs
detection:
  selection:
    UserPrincipalName|contains: '#EXT#@' # Identifies external/guest users
  filter_known_geo:
    Location: 'DE'
  condition: selection and not filter_known_geo
indicators.description: Detects anomalous login patterns from third-party vendor accounts, such as logins from unexpected geographic locations, which could indicate a compromised vendor account being used in a supply chain attack.
